reason: Response B has the characteristic Claude Sonnet register for this kind of supportive-note request: warm but grounded, practical and concrete (mention tutoring services, adult-student programs, asking instructors for help), and measured in tone without straining for literary effect. Response A is more polished and aphoristic ("they are, in fact, the whole game"; "judgment is the rarest thing in any classroom"), reading like a stylized imitation aiming at an essayistic voice, whereas Sonnet typically balances encouragement with actionable, down-to-earth advice as B does.
